rulings.Case Title: COLONIAL MEDICAL GROUP, INC. A CALIFORNIA VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File An Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandamus And/Or Prohibition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF COLONIAL MEDICAL GROUP, INC'S Motion For Leave To File An Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandamus And/Or Prohibition The motion to amend is denied. Petitioner/Plaintiff fails to provide a declaration in compliance with CRC 3.1324(b). Plaintiff/Petitioner does not sufficiently explain why the motion is being brought at this time and allowing amendment at this stage of the proceedings (discovery closed, trial date less than a month away) would prejudice defendants/respondents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT S.F.P.D. CHIEF OF POLICE GEORGE GASCON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend pursuant to CCP sec. 430.10(e). The complaint does not plead facts establishing Plaintiff complied with the mandatory Government Claims Act pre-lawsuit presentation requirements. (Gov. Code sec. 945.4.) The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend because Plaintiff has not stated he could amend the complaint to cure this defect. rulings.Case Title: CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC. VS. A&B PAINTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC'S MOTION For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Granted. Cross-complainant Cahill's cash settlement amount of $500,000 is within the Tech-Bilt v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, "reasonable range" or "ballpark" standard for good faith. Non-settling defendants may receive benefit from the offset of Cahill's settlement contribution, despite its assignment of indemnification rights to plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CARTER et al VS. PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE GROUP LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn In Support Of Mtn Compel Production Of Documents, Compel Deposition, And For A Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFFS ROBERT CARTER AND BOBBIE CARTER'S MOTION In Support Of Mtn Compel Production Of Documents, Compel Deposition, And For A Monetary Sanctions Denied. Plaintiffs neither argue that the PMQ witnesses refused to answer any questions nor identify specific questions or PMQ topics upon which they request relief. Plaintiffs have not established that any of the PMQ witnesses were unprepared or unresponsive. (See CCP § 2025.230.) Plaintiffs' motion to compel production of documents is time-barred. (CCP § 2031.310(c).) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: AUTUMN FELDMEIER VS. WONDERBAR, LLC DBA RED DEVIL LOUNGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (Ntc) By Stanley Anastasio As Trustee Of The 1986 Anastasio Living Trust Dated April 8, 1986 Against Wonderbar, Llc Dba Red Devil Lounge
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT STANLEY ANASTASIO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (Ntc) By Stanley Anastasio As Trustee Of The 1986 Anastasio Living Trust Dated April 8, 1986 Against Wonderbar, Llc Dba Red Devil Lounge Motion for summary adjudication granted. There is no triable issue of material fact as to the contractual indemnification agreement between cross-complainants in relation to the duty to defend. The underlying litigation is embraced by the terms of the indemnity provision, and gives rise to the duty to defend as soon as the claim is made against the indemnitee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TIBOR SZABO VS. MICHAEL VLASTONE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Attendance And Document Production And For Discovery Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT HENRY LAUGHLIN's Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Document Production And For Discovery Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion compelling plaintiff to appear for deposition, produce documents, and pay discovery sanctions is granted. The plaintiff is ordered to appear for deposition on March 14, 2014 at 10:00 am at Clark Reporting and Video Conferencing, 2140 Shattuck, Suite 405, Berkeley, CA 94704. The record reflects that defendant formally set the deposition of the plaintiff for December 17, 2013, January 17, 2014, and February 19, 2014, and plaintiff failed to appear on each occasion. If the plaintiff fails to appear for his deposition on March 14, 2014, absent legal/factual good cause shown by the plaintiff, the Court will consider a terminating sanction for violation of a Discovery Order if such motion is made. The plaintiff shall produce all documents responsive to the deposition notice document-request at the time of said deposition. Failure of the plaintiff to produce said documents at the deposition may result in an evidentiary sanction that the documents may not be introduced as evidence at trial. A formal motion will be required for the Court to consider said evidentiary sanction. Monetary sanctions in the sum of $1,250.00 connected to the filing of this motion are awarded to defendant. Said monetary sanction shall be paid on or before March 17, 2014. The Court has been advised that the plaintiff has filed a late opposition, a courtesy copy of which was not delivered to the Dept 302. The defendant has objected to the Court considering the late opposition. If the plaintiff appears in person at the hearing and brings his opposition pleading, the Court will inquire as to the reason for the late filing and determine whether it should be considered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DOREEN SINGH VS. EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2014, Line 19, DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Continued to March 13, 2014, to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 20, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: LIUDMILA BICHEGKUEVA VS. EXTREME TOWING, INC. et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Action And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2014, Line 20, DEFENDANTS EXTREME TOWING, INC AND VINCENT MARENTES' MOTION To Transfer Action And For Sanctions The motion to change venue is granted. Plaintiff filed this action in the wrong court because the accident occurred and the defendants reside in Santa Clara County. The Court awards defendants $1,380 in fees under CCP 396b(b). Defendants offered to stipulate to change venue. Plaintiff should have filed the action in the proper court and subsequently filed a motion based on the convenience of witnesses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC., A CORPORATION DBA VS. JPMD LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIABILITY COMPANY DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment; Application For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment After Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2014, Line 21, PLAINTIFF BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC., A CORPORATION'S Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment; Application For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment After Hearing Off calendar as untimely pursuant to CCP §§ 484.040 and 1005(b). The Application was served with the Summons and Complaint by substitute service on January 17, 2014. (Proof of Service, filed Jan. 28, 2014, 5b.) Thereafter, the process server mailed the documents on January 21, 2014. (Id. at 5b(4).) Substitute service is deemed complete on the 10th day after mailing. (CCP § 415.20(a).) Plaintiff Birite Restaurant Supply, Inc. did not provide 16 court days notice. (CCP §§ 484.040, 1005(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/EHG
